title: WRAITH Ops Platform — Mission Dashboard, Intelligence Hub & Field Ops Briefing Tool

requirements.text: Create a Mission Management Dashboard where users can create, view, update, and delete covert missions. Each mission must include: codename, status (Active / Compromised / Completed / Aborted), threat level (Low / Elevated / Critical), assigned operatives, mission type (Recon / Cyber / Counter-Terror / In-Person Op), objectives list, and field reports (timestamped log entries). Display missions in a sortable, filterable list with status badges and threat indicators.

quotes: Mission Management Dashboard - Track covert missions, threats, and field reports

acceptanceCriteria: User can create a new mission with all required fields
User can update mission status and threat level
User can append field report entries to a mission
Missions are listed with status badges and threat level indicators
User can filter missions by status and threat level
Missions persist in backend stable storage

requirements.text: Build an Intelligence Hub module with three sub-sections: (a) Asset Profiles — create and manage profiles for field assets/operatives with codename, clearance level, specialization, status, and bio notes; (b) Threat Assessments — create threat entries with subject name, threat category, risk score (1–10), summary, and linked missions; (c) Encrypted Notes — create freeform classified notes with a title, body, classification tag (CONFIDENTIAL / SECRET / TOP SECRET / TS-SCI), and timestamp. All records must be stored and retrievable from the backend.

quotes: Intelligence Hub - asset profiles, threat assessments, and encrypted notes

acceptanceCriteria: User can create, view, edit, and delete asset profiles
User can create, view, edit, and delete threat assessments with a risk score
User can create, view, edit, and delete encrypted notes with classification tags
All three sub-sections are accessible from a tabbed or sectioned UI within the Intelligence Hub
All records persist in backend stable storage

requirements.text: Implement a Field Ops Briefing Tool that allows users to compose and display structured mission briefings. Each briefing must include: operation codename, mission date, lead officer, objectives list (add/remove items), High-Value Target (HVT) profiles (name, description, threat tier), exfil routes (freeform text), rules of engagement, and classification level. Provide a 'Briefing View' mode that renders the briefing as a formatted classified document, suitable for presentation.

quotes: Field Ops Briefing Tool - build and display mission briefings with objectives and HVT profiles

acceptanceCriteria: User can create a new briefing with all required fields
User can add and remove items from the objectives list
User can add HVT profiles with name, description, and threat tier
Briefing View mode renders a formatted, print-ready classified document layout
Briefings are saved and retrievable from the backend
User can edit and delete existing briefings

requirements.text: Apply a cohesive dark classified-ops visual theme across the entire application. Use a near-black background palette with high-contrast text (off-white/amber/red), monospaced or military-style typography, glowing accent elements suggesting digital/terminal aesthetics, classification banner strips at the top of sensitive views, and redaction-style UI components (e.g., blacked-out bars for high-sensitivity fields). Navigation should use a side panel or top bar styled as a classified operations interface. Apply the theme consistently to all three modules.
acceptanceCriteria: All pages use a dark near-black background
Typography is monospaced or military-inspired with amber/red/off-white accents
Classification banner strips appear on sensitive views
Navigation is styled as a classified ops control panel
Theme is applied consistently across all three modules

constraints: All data must be stored in the Motoko backend single actor using stable storage
Do not edit any files under frontend/src/hooks/ or frontend/src/components/ui
No external APIs, databases, or cloud services — ICP backend only
nonGoals: Real authentication beyond Internet Identity (if integrated)
Actual encryption of stored notes (classification tags are cosmetic/organizational)
Map or geolocation features for exfil routes

required: Dark classified agency logo emblem — a stylized wraith/ghost silhouette inside a hexagonal badge with the text 'W.R.A.I.T.H.' and subtle circuit-line details, monochrome with amber glow accents (wraith-logo.dim_256x256.png)
Dark textured background with faint redacted-document scanlines and noise, suitable as a full-page app background (wraith-bg.dim_1920x1080.png)
